behaviors.description: A request whose signature does not match its body is rejected with a 401 response; the body is not forwarded.
behaviors.justification: Real failure is at a module handoff: raw-body/signature verification must gate forwarding to the queue. A pure single-function test can miss wiring mistakes where invalid requests still call enqueue. Rejected higher levels: behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess beyond local module wiring, and does not depend on AI output.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with realistic request object (raw body + bad X-Signature-256) wired to real verifier module and queue adapter test double/spied integration seam; assert 401 and zero enqueue calls.

behaviors.description: An event is written to the persistent queue before the HTTP 200 acknowledgment is returned to the sender.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering across handler and queue modules (state handoff/order propagation), not just local logic. Integration is the minimum to catch ack-before-persist regressions. Rejected System: behavior text requires ordering, not real network/OS effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue implementation seam that records commit timing; send request and assert queue write/commit happens before response completion callback/promise resolves with 200.

behaviors.description: The handler returns the HTTP response without waiting for downstream processing of the event to finish.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between enqueue/dispatch and downstream processor completion; need module interaction to prove non-blocking behavior. Rejected System: no explicit requirement for real OS/network timing artifacts, only async decoupling contract.
behaviors.test_description: Use a downstream processor stub that blocks on a controllable promise; invoke handler and assert HTTP response completes while processor is still blocked, then release processor.

behaviors.description: The response arrives within 3 seconds wall-clock time even when downstream processing of earlier events is slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on wall-clock latency under concurrency, which is sensitive to real runtime scheduling/event-loop contention and HTTP serving behavior. In-process mocks can hide true latency failure modes. Rejected lower levels: wording requires concurrent, time-bounded end-to-end response behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run real Node HTTP service process; generate concurrent load with earlier events triggering slow downstream work; measure actual request-response latency and assert responses stay under 3s wall-clock.

behaviors.description: An event whose ID was already received is not written to the queue a second time.
behaviors.justification: Dedup correctness depends on interaction between handler and persistence/dedup state (cross-module state handoff). Unit-only logic checks can miss duplicate writes caused by repository/queue wiring. Rejected System: no requirement for real OS/network primitives in the behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Send two requests with same event ID through handler wired to real dedup/persistence modules (local test DB/store acceptable); assert first write succeeds and second does not create another queue record.

behaviors.description: When the queue reports it is full, the handler returns 503 with a Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: Failure mode is translation of queue backpressure signal into HTTP status/headers across module boundary. Needs handler+queue wiring, not real infrastructure. Rejected System: behavior says "queue reports it is full" (signal mapping), not "real queue saturation under OS/network conditions."
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler to queue module/test seam that returns a full-capacity error; assert HTTP 503 and presence/value format of Retry-After header.

behaviors.description: The persistent queue actually enforces its configured capacity limit and rejects events that would exceed it.
behaviors.justification: This validates queue component behavior with real local persistence wiring (DB/filesystem) and capacity state transitions; it is beyond a single pure function. Rejected System: behavior does not require external network/subprocess; local persistent store integration is sufficient per level definition.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with real local persistence backend and small configured capacity; enqueue up to limit then one extra; assert overflow enqueue is rejected and persisted count does not exceed configured capacity.
